Overview

Start from the repository root beside [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md). This session did the requested superficial research sweep, identified the main existing anchors for store bootstrap, benchmarking, and tracing/log workflows, then bootstrapped a new repo-level policy tracker with three focused child trackers in the root ticket store so the next session can continue from durable artifacts instead of reconstructing context.

Working Directory

Start in the repository root: use [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) and [./.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md) as the operating guardrails, then work from the new root tracker at [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md).

Findings

- There was no single existing workflow-policy tracker covering all three requested areas. The gap is now captured in [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md).
- The minimal-store/bootstrap problem already has a useful draft spec seed and related workspace-resolution precedent, but not a canonical executable policy. The most relevant seeds are [./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml](./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml) and [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md).
- Benchmarking already has meaningful partial work in `memory-api` and draft transport-matrix spec work, but not one repo workflow explaining executable anchors, load profiles, result capture, and evidence summarization. The strongest anchors are [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md) and [./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md](./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md).
- Tracing/log policy has solid architectural precedent and some completed `log-api` work, but still lacks one operator-facing workflow for what to instrument, where artifacts belong, and how executions/logs/journals relate. The strongest anchors are [./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md), [./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md), and [./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md).

Decisions

- The planning work belongs in the root ticket store, not only under `memory-api`, because the requested workflow policies cross repo boundaries and touch root-store tickets, root-store logs, `memory-api`, and shared instructions.
- The work was intentionally split into one root tracker plus three child trackers so the next session can execute one focused research slice at a time while preserving a single long-horizon umbrella.
- The first session goal was limited to research anchoring and ticket bootstrapping. No spec edits or code changes were made yet because the highest-value next step is to map existing patterns before deciding whether the repo needs new specs, template crates, shared harnesses, or `log-api` feature work.

Blockers

- There is still no validated minimal-store template or fixture proving out-of-the-box CRUD, schema validation, cross-store references, and workspace hierarchy behavior.
- Benchmark behavior is still split across Criterion, custom perf/tracing work, and `test-api` execution capture; the policy layer is missing even though several building blocks exist.
- Full tracing/log workflow policy may be blocked by remaining `log-api` capability gaps; the next session needs to make those blockers explicit instead of assuming the current surfaces are sufficient.

Suggested Goal

- Long-horizon goal: turn store bootstrap, benchmarking, and tracing/logging into durable repository workflows with executable validation and evidence expectations, starting from [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md).

Key Entities

- Ticket `6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871`: [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md)  
  Root tracker created in this session. It now depends on the three focused research trackers and is the main orchestration surface for follow-up handoffs.
- Ticket `79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5`: [./.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/description.md)  
  New minimal-store bootstrap policy tracker. Start here first if the next session is taking the most foundational slice.
- Ticket `a2c469c4-c62b-444d-81ed-6c936bee8ba3`: [./.ticket/tickets/a2c469c4-c62b-444d-81ed-6c936bee8ba3/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/a2c469c4-c62b-444d-81ed-6c936bee8ba3/description.md)  
  New end-to-end benchmark policy tracker. It points at the existing `test-api` and transport-matrix work instead of starting from scratch.
- Ticket `a71c2da8-0972-4c2d-9754-0a0e06db5272`: [./.ticket/tickets/a71c2da8-0972-4c2d-9754-0a0e06db5272/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/a71c2da8-0972-4c2d-9754-0a0e06db5272/description.md)  
  New tracing and `log-api` policy tracker. Use this to reconcile instrumentation expectations with artifact ownership and runtime evidence.
- Ticket `39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9`: [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md)  
  Existing `memory-api` workspace-resolution tracker. It is the clearest current precedent for the “works in a workspace hierarchy out of the box” requirement.
- Ticket `a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759`: [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md)  
  Existing `test-api` benchmark/validation umbrella. Use it to avoid duplicating harness planning and to decide what should stay in `memory-api` versus repo-level workflow policy.
- Ticket `d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6`: [./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md)  
  Completed `log-api` runtime-session and cross-store link precedent. This is the clearest proof that the new tracing/log policy can build on existing durable modeling work.
- Spec `9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030`: [./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml](./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml)  
  Draft spec seed for store bootstrap instructions and slash-skill generation. It is the most obvious candidate for either reuse or refinement when the minimal-store policy becomes concrete.
- Spec `76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b`: [./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md](./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md)  
  Draft transport-layer benchmark strategy. Use it to anchor the executable-anchor and transport-matrix discussion.
- Source anchor: [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs)  
  Concrete example of default schema registration in a domain-owned store.
- Source anchor: [./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs)  
  Concrete example of schema exposure over transport.
- Source anchor: [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs)  
  Concrete schema-validation test surface that can inform minimal-store validation policy.
- Source anchor: [./context-stack/context-api/src/log_parser.rs](./context-stack/context-api/src/log_parser.rs)  
  Concrete tracing/log parsing anchor surfaced during this session for the tracing/log policy track.

Next Actions

1. Check in on the draft board against ticket `79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5` before starting deeper work, because the board was clear during this handoff and the minimal-store track is the best first slice.
2. Read [./.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/description.md), [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/description.md), and [./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml](./.spec/specs/9ee9387f-5384-42a9-95c4-ecbad1713030/spec.toml), then produce a comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
3. First validation check: before leaving the minimal-store slice, create one durable artifact that proves the research advanced the state of the repo, either by updating the bootstrap spec seed or by creating one or more concrete implementation tickets anchored to specific crates/tests.
4. Move to the benchmark slice in [./.ticket/tickets/a2c469c4-c62b-444d-81ed-6c936bee8ba3/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/a2c469c4-c62b-444d-81ed-6c936bee8ba3/description.md), using [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a0bc8bd8-3fe0-4768-a895-b1bacee42759/description.md) and [./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md](./memory-api/.spec/specs/76da5f2d-cea9-49d9-b223-730a0c2a5d6b/body.md) to define the benchmark taxonomy and the first executable-anchor shape.
5. Validate the benchmark slice by mapping at least one existing bench or transport test to the proposed executable-anchor shape and capturing the mismatch list that still blocks unification.
6. Move to the tracing/log slice in [./.ticket/tickets/a71c2da8-0972-4c2d-9754-0a0e06db5272/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/a71c2da8-0972-4c2d-9754-0a0e06db5272/description.md), using [./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/d3349747-b2f2-4dd4-b73c-dc016fec80d6/description.md), [./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/73b2cd22-942b-4205-86e5-333df2373211/description.md), and [./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/2e41c96d-fe9f-4cf2-b941-6f0d452f237c/description.md) to produce an artifact-ownership matrix and explicit blocker list.
7. Update the root tracker at [./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md](./.ticket/tickets/6e72756f-11c6-405f-8d74-0ab608172871/description.md) with the synthesized findings and any newly created child tickets/specs, then continue until all three policy slices are either decomposed into executable follow-up work or explicitly blocked.

Completion Status

- `completed` Bootstrapped the root workflow-policy tracker and linked it to three focused child trackers.
- `completed` Collected the main existing repo anchors for minimal-store bootstrap, benchmark strategy, and tracing/log policy.
- `not-started` Minimal-store comparison matrix and template-boundary decision.
- `not-started` Benchmark taxonomy, executable-anchor schema, and unification decision.
- `not-started` Tracing/log artifact-ownership matrix and blocker map.
- `not-started` Spec updates or follow-up implementation tickets produced from the research.
- `not-started` Root-tracker synthesis pass after the three focused research slices.

Definition Of Done

- The minimal-store, benchmark, and tracing/log child trackers each produce durable outputs, not just notes.
- At least one relevant spec is updated or one or more concrete implementation tickets are created for each slice.
- The repo ends with explicit workflow policy for bootstrap, benchmarking, and tracing/log evidence management, with executable validation expectations and named blockers where implementation cannot yet proceed.

Board And Persisted-Session Note

- The draft board was clear when this handoff was generated. The next session should check in before continuing, preferably on ticket `79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5` first.
- No special transcript artifact needs to be replayed. The durable state to resume from is now in the new ticket descriptions and the linked spec/source anchors above.
- Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

Definition Of Done

- The minimal-store, benchmark, and tracing/log child trackers each produce durable outputs, not just notes.
- At least one relevant spec is updated or one or more concrete implementation tickets are created for each slice.
- The repo ends with explicit workflow policy for bootstrap, benchmarking, and tracing/log evidence management, with executable validation expectations and named blockers where implementation cannot yet proceed.

Board And Persisted-Session Note

-# Summary
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Behavior Story
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Provided Surface Contracts
-
-- Define provided contracts for this behavior slice.
-
-## Required Validation
-
-- Triangulate behavior with executable checks, natural-language clauses, and code/schema/API references when available.
-
-## Related Implementation Tickets
-
-- No related implementation ticket is linked yet.
-
-## Background Knowledge References
-
-- Prefer entity references and context rendering over embedding fully expanded payloads in this spec body.
-
-## Legacy Content (Preserved)
-
-# Problem
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Goals
-
-- Encode domain-store bootstrap guidance in canonical rule entries and generated instruction/prompt surfaces.
-- Provide a slash command skill that takes one prompt and scaffolds a minimal domain store.
-- Add automated end-to-end prompt regression tests that verify generated outputs and architecture conformance.
-
-## Required behavior
-
-### Rule-managed source of truth
-- Instruction and prompt artifacts must be generated from rule-store entries.
-- Generated files are not hand-maintained; regeneration is part of validation.
-- Rule entries explicitly encode architecture constraints from `target/tmp/architecture-decisions.md` and `architecture/cross-store-workspace-interaction`.
-
-### Generated asset ownership
-- Canonical source ownership: rule-store entries.
-- Generated targets: slash-skill prompt assets, bootstrap instruction files, and template fragments.
-- Manual edits to generated targets are treated as drift and must fail drift checks.
-
-### Slash command scaffolding flow
-- Single prompt input yields a minimal store scaffold including crate manifest, base models, storage API shell, CLI/MCP/HTTP stubs where applicable, and baseline tests.
-- Output includes contract-crate and composition-root guidance so domain crates remain decoupled and DAG-safe.
-- The flow emits actionable next steps for completing domain-specific logic.
-
-### Prompt contract
-- Required prompt fields: domain name, primary entities, required interfaces, optional external integrations.
-- Prompt parser reports normalized intent and explicit warnings for ambiguous or conflicting instructions.
-- Generated scaffold includes machine-readable manifest of created files and architecture checks performed.
-
-### Architecture conformance checks
-- Generated scaffold must satisfy DAG and layering constraints:
-  - no domain cycles
-  - contract crates remain lightweight
-  - composition root stays in binaries
-- The scaffolder surfaces conformance warnings when prompt intent conflicts with architecture constraints.
-
-### Operational guardrails
-- rollout supports feature-flag staged enablement (off, preview, controlled, general).
-- failed drift/replay/e2e gates trigger deterministic rollback behavior and preserve diagnostic artifacts.
-- operator diagnostics include clear next-step remediation guidance for failed scaffold generations.
-
-### Regression harness
-- A dedicated E2E prompt test suite validates representative prompts and asserts:
-  - generated files compile
-  - baseline tests run
-  - architecture checks pass
-  - generated instructions/prompt outputs are stable or intentionally versioned
-
-## Validation-track decomposition
-
-- Validation track 1: generation-drift checks ensure rule sources and generated instruction/prompt targets remain synchronized.
-- Validation track 2: prompt replay matrix validates scaffold behavior across simple, medium, complex, and edge-case prompt families.
-- Validation track 3: end-to-end harness aggregates compile/test/conformance checks across the replay suite and reports regressions with actionable diffs.
-
-## Rollout gates
-
-1. Drift-gate: source and generated artifacts remain synchronized.
-2. Replay-gate: prompt matrix remains stable across representative prompt classes.
-3. E2E-gate: generated scaffolds compile, tests pass, and architecture checks pass.
-
-Downstream scaffold rollout is blocked if any gate fails.
-
-## Major risks
-
-- prompt ambiguity yielding unusable scaffolds without diagnostics
-- drift between rule-source guidance and generated slash-command behavior
-- regression suite brittleness from unstable output ordering
-
-## Acceptance criteria
-
-- rule-source and generated-target workflow is explicit and validated
-- slash-command scaffold contract is explicit and architecture-constrained
-- E2E prompt regression tickets and validation requirements are present
-- rollout gating order across drift checks, prompt replay, and full E2E harness is explicit
-
-## Traceability
-
-- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
-- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
-- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
-- [dedac9f5 [validation] Rule-target generation drift checks for scaffold guidance assets](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/dedac9f5-0d4d-4ad0-8a7e-4acd361c273e/ticket.toml)
-- [2ff2c8e8 [validation] Prompt replay matrix for scaffold skill domain coverage](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/2ff2c8e8-eaec-4bd9-9312-ae13cd4b243a/ticket.toml)
-- [70222986 [validation] E2E regression harness for domain-store scaffold prompts](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/70222986-3325-4d45-892e-31e7f4d09aa6/ticket.toml)
-- [a87dcdf9 [scaffold] Rollout guardrails, feature flags, and rollback protocol](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/a87dcdf9-0638-4c84-a4ed-c8f4d3518e72/ticket.toml)
-
-## Validation
-
-- rule sync-targets check passes for generated instruction/prompt outputs
-- focused tests for slash-command prompt parsing and scaffold generation
-- drift-check suite validates source-to-generated alignment
-- replay matrix suite validates behavior across prompt classes
-- E2E prompt suite validates compile/test/architecture checks across prompt fixtures
+# Summary
+
+Creating new domain stores repeatedly is still slow because the canonical bootstrap contract is scattered across existing domains. This spec now defines the minimal-store policy first, so later scaffolding or slash-skill automation can generate against a stable contract instead of guessing from prior implementations.
+
+## Behavior Story
+
+An engineer starting a new memory-backed domain store should be able to identify the smallest required handwritten surfaces, the shared `memory-api` capabilities they can reuse, the transport exposure pattern they need to follow, and the executable checks that prove the store works in a nested workspace with schema validation and cross-store references.
+
+## Provided Surface Contracts
+
+- The repository owns one canonical bootstrap policy for entity-store domains built on `memory_api::storage::EntityStore`.
+- The policy distinguishes entity-store domains from session-style artifact stores so scaffold work does not force both patterns into one template.
+- Every entity-store bootstrap must document: schema registration, store-open path, workspace-resolution path, transport exposure, schema-validation tests, and cross-store reference expectations.
+- Prompt-driven scaffolding is a downstream automation layer and must generate from this policy rather than inventing structure.
+
+## Bootstrap Comparison Matrix
+
+| Domain | Bootstrap primitive | Schema or config bootstrap | Transport or operator exposure | Validation anchor | Reuse signal |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `ticket-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Built-in `TicketTypeSchema` values in `model/default_schema.rs`; workflow states and edge rules are domain-owned | CLI, MCP, and HTTP all expose the same store and schema surfaces; HTTP `schema.rs` is the clearest wire-format example | `tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs` plus transport parity work | Richest end-to-end precedent for schema, workflow, and transport parity |
+| `spec-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Single embedded `EntityTypeSchema` in `default_schema.rs` registered into a `SchemaRegistry` | CLI and MCP patterns stay close to the store with minimal extra workflow logic | `crates/spec-api/tests/schema_test.rs` | Closest example of the smallest entity-store bootstrap |
+| `rule-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Multiple embedded schemas registered in `rule_schema_registry()` | Operator-facing behavior stays store-centric and extends into generated-target workflows rather than a broad workflow engine | `crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs` and rule-store tests | Best example of a domain with more than one built-in entity schema |
+| `session-api` | Dedicated session artifact store and planning layer | `SessionStoreConfig` and `SessionStorePlan` derive paths and layout; no generic `SchemaRegistry` bootstrap | Hook binaries and MCP-side session workflows operate on session captures and artifacts instead of entity CRUD | `crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs` and capture-hook E2E tests | Distinct pattern; document it, but keep it out of the first entity-store template |
+
+## Template Boundary Decision
+
+### First template target
+
+The first canonical bootstrap template targets entity-store domains such as ticket, spec, rule, test, log, and similar stores that open through shared `memory-api` storage primitives.
+
+### Explicit non-goal for phase 1
+
+`session-api` remains a related but separate bootstrap pattern because it is config-driven artifact storage rather than a schema-registered entity store. The policy must reference it as a contrast case, not as a required output of the first template.
+
+### Handwritten vs generated boundary
+
+- Handwritten, domain-owned surfaces:
+  - entity schemas and semantic field rules
+  - domain store wrapper and query semantics
+  - cross-store reference vocabulary and evidence expectations
+  - transport-specific composition when a domain needs bespoke workflow or wire formats
+- Candidate generated surfaces:
+  - crate skeleton and manifest wiring
+  - default schema registration module
+  - store open/create shell using shared `memory-api` primitives
+  - CLI, MCP, and optional HTTP stub entry points
+  - baseline CRUD, schema-validation, and workspace-resolution tests
+
+## Required Validation
+
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p spec-api`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p ticket-cli --test contracts_schema_validation`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p session-api`
+- Focused workspace-resolution validation against the `memory-api` resolver path so new stores inherit nested-workspace behavior rather than re-implementing it.
+- At least one bootstrap fixture or template test must prove: create, get, update, delete, schema validation, and workspace-root discovery out of the box.
+
+## Related Implementation Tickets
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+
+## Background Knowledge References
+
+- `memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs`
+- `memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs`
+
+## Legacy Content (Preserved)
+
+The original slash-skill and generated-guidance idea remains valid, but it is now downstream of the minimal-store policy. Generated prompt assets, drift checks, replay suites, and E2E scaffold testing should advance only after the repository agrees on the phase-1 entity-store template boundary defined above.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The repository has an explicit comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
+- The phase-1 template boundary clearly targets entity stores and explicitly excludes the session-artifact pattern.
+- Required bootstrap validation includes schema validation, CRUD, and nested-workspace resolution rather than compile-only checks.
+- Follow-up scaffold automation is described as a consumer of the policy, not the policy itself.
+
+## Traceability
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
+- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
+
+## Validation
+
+- Focused package tests for `spec-api`, `ticket-cli`, and `session-api` remain green.
+- Future scaffold generation work must add a template or fixture test that proves CRUD, schema validation, and nested workspace discovery without domain-specific patching.
*** End Patch

-- Add automated end-to-end prompt regression tests that verify generated outputs and architecture conformance.
-
-## Required behavior
-
-### Rule-managed source of truth
-- Instruction and prompt artifacts must be generated from rule-store entries.
-- Generated files are not hand-maintained; regeneration is part of validation.
-- Rule entries explicitly encode architecture constraints from `target/tmp/architecture-decisions.md` and `architecture/cross-store-workspace-interaction`.
-
-### Generated asset ownership
-- Canonical source ownership: rule-store entries.
-- Generated targets: slash-skill prompt assets, bootstrap instruction files, and template fragments.
-- Manual edits to generated targets are treated as drift and must fail drift checks.
-
-### Slash command scaffolding flow
-- Single prompt input yields a minimal store scaffold including crate manifest, base models, storage API shell, CLI/MCP/HTTP stubs where applicable, and baseline tests.
-- Output includes contract-crate and composition-root guidance so domain crates remain decoupled and DAG-safe.
-- The flow emits actionable next steps for completing domain-specific logic.
-
-### Prompt contract
-- Required prompt fields: domain name, primary entities, required interfaces, optional external integrations.
-- Prompt parser reports normalized intent and explicit warnings for ambiguous or conflicting instructions.
-- Generated scaffold includes machine-readable manifest of created files and architecture checks performed.
-
-### Architecture conformance checks
-- Generated scaffold must satisfy DAG and layering constraints:
-  - no domain cycles
-  - contract crates remain lightweight
-  - composition root stays in binaries
-- The scaffolder surfaces conformance warnings when prompt intent conflicts with architecture constraints.
-
-### Operational guardrails
-- rollout supports feature-flag staged enablement (off, preview, controlled, general).
-- failed drift/replay/e2e gates trigger deterministic rollback behavior and preserve diagnostic artifacts.
-- operator diagnostics include clear next-step remediation guidance for failed scaffold generations.
-
-### Regression harness
-- A dedicated E2E prompt test suite validates representative prompts and asserts:
-  - generated files compile
-  - baseline tests run
-  - architecture checks pass
-  - generated instructions/prompt outputs are stable or intentionally versioned
-
-## Validation-track decomposition
-
-- Validation track 1: generation-drift checks ensure rule sources and generated instruction/prompt targets remain synchronized.
-- Validation track 2: prompt replay matrix validates scaffold behavior across simple, medium, complex, and edge-case prompt families.
-- Validation track 3: end-to-end harness aggregates compile/test/conformance checks across the replay suite and reports regressions with actionable diffs.
-
-## Rollout gates
-
-1. Drift-gate: source and generated artifacts remain synchronized.
-2. Replay-gate: prompt matrix remains stable across representative prompt classes.
-3. E2E-gate: generated scaffolds compile, tests pass, and architecture checks pass.
-
-Downstream scaffold rollout is blocked if any gate fails.
-
-## Major risks
-
-- prompt ambiguity yielding unusable scaffolds without diagnostics
-- drift between rule-source guidance and generated slash-command behavior
-- regression suite brittleness from unstable output ordering
-
-## Acceptance criteria
-
-- rule-source and generated-target workflow is explicit and validated
-- slash-command scaffold contract is explicit and architecture-constrained
-- E2E prompt regression tickets and validation requirements are present
-- rollout gating order across drift checks, prompt replay, and full E2E harness is explicit
-
-## Traceability
-

-- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)

+# Summary
+
+Creating new domain stores repeatedly is still slow because the canonical bootstrap contract is scattered across existing domains. This spec now defines the minimal-store policy first, so later scaffolding or slash-skill automation can generate against a stable contract instead of guessing from prior implementations.
+
+## Behavior Story
+
+An engineer starting a new memory-backed domain store should be able to identify the smallest required handwritten surfaces, the shared `memory-api` capabilities they can reuse, the transport exposure pattern they need to follow, and the executable checks that prove the store works in a nested workspace with schema validation and cross-store references.
+
+## Provided Surface Contracts
+
+- The repository owns one canonical bootstrap policy for entity-store domains built on `memory_api::storage::EntityStore`.
+- The policy distinguishes entity-store domains from session-style artifact stores so scaffold work does not force both patterns into one template.
+- Every entity-store bootstrap must document: schema registration, store-open path, workspace-resolution path, transport exposure, schema-validation tests, and cross-store reference expectations.
+- Prompt-driven scaffolding is a downstream automation layer and must generate from this policy rather than inventing structure.
+
+## Bootstrap Comparison Matrix
+
+| Domain | Bootstrap primitive | Schema or config bootstrap | Transport or operator exposure | Validation anchor | Reuse signal |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `ticket-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Built-in `TicketTypeSchema` values in `model/default_schema.rs`; workflow states and edge rules are domain-owned | CLI, MCP, and HTTP all expose the same store and schema surfaces; HTTP `schema.rs` is the clearest wire-format example | `tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs` plus transport parity work | Richest end-to-end precedent for schema, workflow, and transport parity |
+| `spec-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Single embedded `EntityTypeSchema` in `default_schema.rs` registered into a `SchemaRegistry` | CLI and MCP patterns stay close to the store with minimal extra workflow logic | `crates/spec-api/tests/schema_test.rs` | Closest example of the smallest entity-store bootstrap |
+| `rule-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Multiple embedded schemas registered in `rule_schema_registry()` | Operator-facing behavior stays store-centric and extends into generated-target workflows rather than a broad workflow engine | `crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs` and rule-store tests | Best example of a domain with more than one built-in entity schema |
+| `session-api` | Dedicated session artifact store and planning layer | `SessionStoreConfig` and `SessionStorePlan` derive paths and layout; no generic `SchemaRegistry` bootstrap | Hook binaries and MCP-side session workflows operate on session captures and artifacts instead of entity CRUD | `crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs` and capture-hook E2E tests | Distinct pattern; document it, but keep it out of the first entity-store template |
+
+## Template Boundary Decision
+
+### First template target
+
+The first canonical bootstrap template targets entity-store domains such as ticket, spec, rule, test, log, and similar stores that open through shared `memory-api` storage primitives.
+
+### Explicit non-goal for phase 1
+
+`session-api` remains a related but separate bootstrap pattern because it is config-driven artifact storage rather than a schema-registered entity store. The policy must reference it as a contrast case, not as a required output of the first template.
+
+### Handwritten vs generated boundary
+
+- Handwritten, domain-owned surfaces:
+  - entity schemas and semantic field rules
+  - domain store wrapper and query semantics
+  - cross-store reference vocabulary and evidence expectations
+  - transport-specific composition when a domain needs bespoke workflow or wire formats
+- Candidate generated surfaces:
+  - crate skeleton and manifest wiring
+  - default schema registration module
+  - store open/create shell using shared `memory-api` primitives
+  - CLI, MCP, and optional HTTP stub entry points
+  - baseline CRUD, schema-validation, and workspace-resolution tests
+
+## Required Validation
+
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p spec-api`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p ticket-cli --test contracts_schema_validation`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p session-api`
+- Focused workspace-resolution validation against the `memory-api` resolver path so new stores inherit nested-workspace behavior rather than re-implementing it.
+- At least one bootstrap fixture or template test must prove: create, get, update, delete, schema validation, and workspace-root discovery out of the box.
+
+## Related Implementation Tickets
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+
+## Background Knowledge References
+
+- `memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs`
+- `memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs`
+
+## Legacy Content (Preserved)
+
+The original slash-skill and generated-guidance idea remains valid, but it is now downstream of the minimal-store policy. Generated prompt assets, drift checks, replay suites, and E2E scaffold testing should advance only after the repository agrees on the phase-1 entity-store template boundary defined above.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The repository has an explicit comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
+- The phase-1 template boundary clearly targets entity stores and explicitly excludes the session-artifact pattern.
+- Required bootstrap validation includes schema validation, CRUD, and nested-workspace resolution rather than compile-only checks.
+- Follow-up scaffold automation is described as a consumer of the policy, not the policy itself.
+
+## Traceability
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
+- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
+
+## Validation
+
+- Focused package tests for `spec-api`, `ticket-cli`, and `session-api` remain green.
+- Future scaffold generation work must add a template or fixture test that proves CRUD, schema validation, and nested workspace discovery without domain-specific patching.
*** End Patch

-# Summary
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Behavior Story
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Provided Surface Contracts
-
-- Define provided contracts for this behavior slice.
-
-## Required Validation
-
-- Triangulate behavior with executable checks, natural-language clauses, and code/schema/API references when available.
-
-## Related Implementation Tickets
-
-- No related implementation ticket is linked yet.
-
-## Background Knowledge References
-
-- Prefer entity references and context rendering over embedding fully expanded payloads in this spec body.
-
-## Legacy Content (Preserved)
-
-# Problem
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Goals
-
-- Encode domain-store bootstrap guidance in canonical rule entries and generated instruction/prompt surfaces.
-- Provide a slash command skill that takes one prompt and scaffolds a minimal domain store.
-- Add automated end-to-end prompt regression tests that verify generated outputs and architecture conformance.
-
-## Required behavior
-
-### Rule-managed source of truth
-- Instruction and prompt artifacts must be generated from rule-store entries.
-- Generated files are not hand-maintained; regeneration is part of validation.
-- Rule entries explicitly encode architecture constraints from `target/tmp/architecture-decisions.md` and `architecture/cross-store-workspace-interaction`.
-
-### Generated asset ownership
-- Canonical source ownership: rule-store entries.
-- Generated targets: slash-skill prompt assets, bootstrap instruction files, and template fragments.
-- Manual edits to generated targets are treated as drift and must fail drift checks.
-
-### Slash command scaffolding flow
-- Single prompt input yields a minimal store scaffold including crate manifest, base models, storage API shell, CLI/MCP/HTTP stubs where applicable, and baseline tests.
-- Output includes contract-crate and composition-root guidance so domain crates remain decoupled and DAG-safe.
-- The flow emits actionable next steps for completing domain-specific logic.
-
-### Prompt contract
-- Required prompt fields: domain name, primary entities, required interfaces, optional external integrations.
-- Prompt parser reports normalized intent and explicit warnings for ambiguous or conflicting instructions.
-- Generated scaffold includes machine-readable manifest of created files and architecture checks performed.
-
-### Architecture conformance checks
-- Generated scaffold must satisfy DAG and layering constraints:
-  - no domain cycles
-  - contract crates remain lightweight
-  - composition root stays in binaries
-- The scaffolder surfaces conformance warnings when prompt intent conflicts with architecture constraints.
-
-### Operational guardrails
-- rollout supports feature-flag staged enablement (off, preview, controlled, general).
-- failed drift/replay/e2e gates trigger deterministic rollback behavior and preserve diagnostic artifacts.
-- operator diagnostics include clear next-step remediation guidance for failed scaffold generations.
-
-### Regression harness
-- A dedicated E2E prompt test suite validates representative prompts and asserts:
-  - generated files compile
-  - baseline tests run
-  - architecture checks pass
-  - generated instructions/prompt outputs are stable or intentionally versioned
-
-## Validation-track decomposition
-
-- Validation track 1: generation-drift checks ensure rule sources and generated instruction/prompt targets remain synchronized.
-- Validation track 2: prompt replay matrix validates scaffold behavior across simple, medium, complex, and edge-case prompt families.
-- Validation track 3: end-to-end harness aggregates compile/test/conformance checks across the replay suite and reports regressions with actionable diffs.
-
-## Rollout gates
-
-1. Drift-gate: source and generated artifacts remain synchronized.
-2. Replay-gate: prompt matrix remains stable across representative prompt classes.
-3. E2E-gate: generated scaffolds compile, tests pass, and architecture checks pass.
-
-Downstream scaffold rollout is blocked if any gate fails.
-
-## Major risks
-
-- prompt ambiguity yielding unusable scaffolds without diagnostics
-- drift between rule-source guidance and generated slash-command behavior
-- regression suite brittleness from unstable output ordering
-
-## Acceptance criteria
-
-- rule-source and generated-target workflow is explicit and validated
-- slash-command scaffold contract is explicit and architecture-constrained
-- E2E prompt regression tickets and validation requirements are present
-- rollout gating order across drift checks, prompt replay, and full E2E harness is explicit
-
-## Traceability
-
-- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
-- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
-- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
-- [dedac9f5 [validation] Rule-target generation drift checks for scaffold guidance assets](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/dedac9f5-0d4d-4ad0-8a7e-4acd361c273e/ticket.toml)
-- [2ff2c8e8 [validation] Prompt replay matrix for scaffold skill domain coverage](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/2ff2c8e8-eaec-4bd9-9312-ae13cd4b243a/ticket.toml)
-- [70222986 [validation] E2E regression harness for domain-store scaffold prompts](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/70222986-3325-4d45-892e-31e7f4d09aa6/ticket.toml)
-- [a87dcdf9 [scaffold] Rollout guardrails, feature flags, and rollback protocol](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/a87dcdf9-0638-4c84-a4ed-c8f4d3518e72/ticket.toml)
-
-## Validation
-
-- rule sync-targets check passes for generated instruction/prompt outputs
-- focused tests for slash-command prompt parsing and scaffold generation
-- drift-check suite validates source-to-generated alignment
-- replay matrix suite validates behavior across prompt classes
-- E2E prompt suite validates compile/test/architecture checks across prompt fixtures
+# Summary
+
+Creating new domain stores repeatedly is still slow because the canonical bootstrap contract is scattered across existing domains. This spec now defines the minimal-store policy first, so later scaffolding or slash-skill automation can generate against a stable contract instead of guessing from prior implementations.
+
+## Behavior Story
+
+An engineer starting a new memory-backed domain store should be able to identify the smallest required handwritten surfaces, the shared `memory-api` capabilities they can reuse, the transport exposure pattern they need to follow, and the executable checks that prove the store works in a nested workspace with schema validation and cross-store references.
+
+## Provided Surface Contracts
+
+- The repository owns one canonical bootstrap policy for entity-store domains built on `memory_api::storage::EntityStore`.
+- The policy distinguishes entity-store domains from session-style artifact stores so scaffold work does not force both patterns into one template.
+- Every entity-store bootstrap must document: schema registration, store-open path, workspace-resolution path, transport exposure, schema-validation tests, and cross-store reference expectations.
+- Prompt-driven scaffolding is a downstream automation layer and must generate from this policy rather than inventing structure.
+
+## Bootstrap Comparison Matrix
+
+| Domain | Bootstrap primitive | Schema or config bootstrap | Transport or operator exposure | Validation anchor | Reuse signal |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `ticket-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Built-in `TicketTypeSchema` values in `model/default_schema.rs`; workflow states and edge rules are domain-owned | CLI, MCP, and HTTP all expose the same store and schema surfaces; HTTP `schema.rs` is the clearest wire-format example | `tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs` plus transport parity work | Richest end-to-end precedent for schema, workflow, and transport parity |
+| `spec-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Single embedded `EntityTypeSchema` in `default_schema.rs` registered into a `SchemaRegistry` | CLI and MCP patterns stay close to the store with minimal extra workflow logic | `crates/spec-api/tests/schema_test.rs` | Closest example of the smallest entity-store bootstrap |
+| `rule-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Multiple embedded schemas registered in `rule_schema_registry()` | Operator-facing behavior stays store-centric and extends into generated-target workflows rather than a broad workflow engine | `crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs` and rule-store tests | Best example of a domain with more than one built-in entity schema |
+| `session-api` | Dedicated session artifact store and planning layer | `SessionStoreConfig` and `SessionStorePlan` derive paths and layout; no generic `SchemaRegistry` bootstrap | Hook binaries and MCP-side session workflows operate on session captures and artifacts instead of entity CRUD | `crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs` and capture-hook E2E tests | Distinct pattern; document it, but keep it out of the first entity-store template |
+
+## Template Boundary Decision
+
+### First template target
+
+The first canonical bootstrap template targets entity-store domains such as ticket, spec, rule, test, log, and similar stores that open through shared `memory-api` storage primitives.
+
+### Explicit non-goal for phase 1
+
+`session-api` remains a related but separate bootstrap pattern because it is config-driven artifact storage rather than a schema-registered entity store. The policy must reference it as a contrast case, not as a required output of the first template.
+
+### Handwritten vs generated boundary
+
+- Handwritten, domain-owned surfaces:
+  - entity schemas and semantic field rules
+  - domain store wrapper and query semantics
+  - cross-store reference vocabulary and evidence expectations
+  - transport-specific composition when a domain needs bespoke workflow or wire formats
+- Candidate generated surfaces:
+  - crate skeleton and manifest wiring
+  - default schema registration module
+  - store open/create shell using shared `memory-api` primitives
+  - CLI, MCP, and optional HTTP stub entry points
+  - baseline CRUD, schema-validation, and workspace-resolution tests
+
+## Required Validation
+
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p spec-api`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p ticket-cli --test contracts_schema_validation`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p session-api`
+- Focused workspace-resolution validation against the `memory-api` resolver path so new stores inherit nested-workspace behavior rather than re-implementing it.
+- At least one bootstrap fixture or template test must prove: create, get, update, delete, schema validation, and workspace-root discovery out of the box.
+
+## Related Implementation Tickets
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+
+## Background Knowledge References
+
+- `memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs`
+- `memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs`
+
+## Legacy Content (Preserved)
+
+The original slash-skill and generated-guidance idea remains valid, but it is now downstream of the minimal-store policy. Generated prompt assets, drift checks, replay suites, and E2E scaffold testing should advance only after the repository agrees on the phase-1 entity-store template boundary defined above.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The repository has an explicit comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
+- The phase-1 template boundary clearly targets entity stores and explicitly excludes the session-artifact pattern.
+- Required bootstrap validation includes schema validation, CRUD, and nested-workspace resolution rather than compile-only checks.
+- Follow-up scaffold automation is described as a consumer of the policy, not the policy itself.
+
+## Traceability
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
+- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
+
+## Validation
+
+- Focused package tests for `spec-api`, `ticket-cli`, and `session-api` remain green.
+- Future scaffold generation work must add a template or fixture test that proves CRUD, schema validation, and nested workspace discovery without domain-specific patching.
*** End Patch
tool_arguments_json.explanation: Refine the draft bootstrap spec into a concrete minimal-store policy artifact with a four-domain comparison matrix and validation recipe.

-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.

-## Behavior Story
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Provided Surface Contracts
-
-- Define provided contracts for this behavior slice.
-

-

-
-- Prefer entity references and context rendering over embedding fully expanded payloads in this spec body.
-
-## Legacy Content (Preserved)
-
-# Problem
-
-Creating new domain stores repeatedly is slow and inconsistent. Teams need one prompt-driven bootstrap flow that generates a minimally functional store while preserving architecture constraints and repository conventions.
-
-## Goals
-
-- Encode domain-store bootstrap guidance in canonical rule entries and generated instruction/prompt surfaces.
-- Provide a slash command skill that takes one prompt and scaffolds a minimal domain store.
-- Add automated end-to-end prompt regression tests that verify generated outputs and architecture conformance.
-
-## Required behavior
-
-### Rule-managed source of truth
-- Instruction and prompt artifacts must be generated from rule-store entries.
-- Generated files are not hand-maintained; regeneration is part of validation.
-- Rule entries explicitly encode architecture constraints from `target/tmp/architecture-decisions.md` and `architecture/cross-store-workspace-interaction`.
-
-### Generated asset ownership
-- Canonical source ownership: rule-store entries.
-- Generated targets: slash-skill prompt assets, bootstrap instruction files, and template fragments.
-- Manual edits to generated targets are treated as drift and must fail drift checks.
-
-### Slash command scaffolding flow
-- Single prompt input yields a minimal store scaffold including crate manifest, base models, storage API shell, CLI/MCP/HTTP stubs where applicable, and baseline tests.
-- Output includes contract-crate and composition-root guidance so domain crates remain decoupled and DAG-safe.
-- The flow emits actionable next steps for completing domain-specific logic.
-
-### Prompt contract
-- Required prompt fields: domain name, primary entities, required interfaces, optional external integrations.
-- Prompt parser reports normalized intent and explicit warnings for ambiguous or conflicting instructions.
-- Generated scaffold includes machine-readable manifest of created files and architecture checks performed.
-
-### Architecture conformance checks
-- Generated scaffold must satisfy DAG and layering constraints:
-  - no domain cycles
-  - contract crates remain lightweight
-  - composition root stays in binaries
-- The scaffolder surfaces conformance warnings when prompt intent conflicts with architecture constraints.
-
-### Operational guardrails
-- rollout supports feature-flag staged enablement (off, preview, controlled, general).
-- failed drift/replay/e2e gates trigger deterministic rollback behavior and preserve diagnostic artifacts.
-- operator diagnostics include clear next-step remediation guidance for failed scaffold generations.
-
-### Regression harness
-- A dedicated E2E prompt test suite validates representative prompts and asserts:
-  - generated files compile
-  - baseline tests run
-  - architecture checks pass
-  - generated instructions/prompt outputs are stable or intentionally versioned
-
-## Validation-track decomposition
-
-- Validation track 1: generation-drift checks ensure rule sources and generated instruction/prompt targets remain synchronized.
-- Validation track 2: prompt replay matrix validates scaffold behavior across simple, medium, complex, and edge-case prompt families.
-- Validation track 3: end-to-end harness aggregates compile/test/conformance checks across the replay suite and reports regressions with actionable diffs.
-
-## Rollout gates
-
-1. Drift-gate: source and generated artifacts remain synchronized.
-2. Replay-gate: prompt matrix remains stable across representative prompt classes.
-3. E2E-gate: generated scaffolds compile, tests pass, and architecture checks pass.
-
-Downstream scaffold rollout is blocked if any gate fails.
-
-## Major risks
-
-- prompt ambiguity yielding unusable scaffolds without diagnostics
-- drift between rule-source guidance and generated slash-command behavior
-- regression suite brittleness from unstable output ordering
-
-## Acceptance criteria
-
-- rule-source and generated-target workflow is explicit and validated
-- slash-command scaffold contract is explicit and architecture-constrained
-- E2E prompt regression tickets and validation requirements are present
-- rollout gating order across drift checks, prompt replay, and full E2E harness is explicit
-
-## Traceability
-

-- rule sync-targets check passes for generated instruction/prompt outputs

+# Summary
+
+Creating new domain stores repeatedly is still slow because the canonical bootstrap contract is scattered across existing domains. This spec now defines the minimal-store policy first, so later scaffolding or slash-skill automation can generate against a stable contract instead of guessing from prior implementations.
+
+## Behavior Story
+
+An engineer starting a new memory-backed domain store should be able to identify the smallest required handwritten surfaces, the shared `memory-api` capabilities they can reuse, the transport exposure pattern they need to follow, and the executable checks that prove the store works in a nested workspace with schema validation and cross-store references.
+
+## Provided Surface Contracts
+
+- The repository owns one canonical bootstrap policy for entity-store domains built on `memory_api::storage::EntityStore`.
+- The policy distinguishes entity-store domains from session-style artifact stores so scaffold work does not force both patterns into one template.
+- Every entity-store bootstrap must document: schema registration, store-open path, workspace-resolution path, transport exposure, schema-validation tests, and cross-store reference expectations.
+- Prompt-driven scaffolding is a downstream automation layer and must generate from this policy rather than inventing structure.
+
+## Bootstrap Comparison Matrix
+
+| Domain | Bootstrap primitive | Schema or config bootstrap | Transport or operator exposure | Validation anchor | Reuse signal |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `ticket-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Built-in `TicketTypeSchema` values in `model/default_schema.rs`; workflow states and edge rules are domain-owned | CLI, MCP, and HTTP all expose the same store and schema surfaces; HTTP `schema.rs` is the clearest wire-format example | `tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs` plus transport parity work | Richest end-to-end precedent for schema, workflow, and transport parity |
+| `spec-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Single embedded `EntityTypeSchema` in `default_schema.rs` registered into a `SchemaRegistry` | CLI and MCP patterns stay close to the store with minimal extra workflow logic | `crates/spec-api/tests/schema_test.rs` | Closest example of the smallest entity-store bootstrap |
+| `rule-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Multiple embedded schemas registered in `rule_schema_registry()` | Operator-facing behavior stays store-centric and extends into generated-target workflows rather than a broad workflow engine | `crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs` and rule-store tests | Best example of a domain with more than one built-in entity schema |
+| `session-api` | Dedicated session artifact store and planning layer | `SessionStoreConfig` and `SessionStorePlan` derive paths and layout; no generic `SchemaRegistry` bootstrap | Hook binaries and MCP-side session workflows operate on session captures and artifacts instead of entity CRUD | `crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs` and capture-hook E2E tests | Distinct pattern; document it, but keep it out of the first entity-store template |
+
+## Template Boundary Decision
+
+### First template target
+
+The first canonical bootstrap template targets entity-store domains such as ticket, spec, rule, test, log, and similar stores that open through shared `memory-api` storage primitives.
+
+### Explicit non-goal for phase 1
+
+`session-api` remains a related but separate bootstrap pattern because it is config-driven artifact storage rather than a schema-registered entity store. The policy must reference it as a contrast case, not as a required output of the first template.
+
+### Handwritten vs generated boundary
+
+- Handwritten, domain-owned surfaces:
+  - entity schemas and semantic field rules
+  - domain store wrapper and query semantics
+  - cross-store reference vocabulary and evidence expectations
+  - transport-specific composition when a domain needs bespoke workflow or wire formats
+- Candidate generated surfaces:
+  - crate skeleton and manifest wiring
+  - default schema registration module
+  - store open/create shell using shared `memory-api` primitives
+  - CLI, MCP, and optional HTTP stub entry points
+  - baseline CRUD, schema-validation, and workspace-resolution tests
+
+## Required Validation
+
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p spec-api`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p ticket-cli --test contracts_schema_validation`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p session-api`
+- Focused workspace-resolution validation against the `memory-api` resolver path so new stores inherit nested-workspace behavior rather than re-implementing it.
+- At least one bootstrap fixture or template test must prove: create, get, update, delete, schema validation, and workspace-root discovery out of the box.
+
+## Related Implementation Tickets
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+
+## Background Knowledge References
+
+- `memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs`
+- `memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs`
+
+## Legacy Content (Preserved)
+
+The original slash-skill and generated-guidance idea remains valid, but it is now downstream of the minimal-store policy. Generated prompt assets, drift checks, replay suites, and E2E scaffold testing should advance only after the repository agrees on the phase-1 entity-store template boundary defined above.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The repository has an explicit comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
+- The phase-1 template boundary clearly targets entity stores and explicitly excludes the session-artifact pattern.
+- Required bootstrap validation includes schema validation, CRUD, and nested-workspace resolution rather than compile-only checks.
+- Follow-up scaffold automation is described as a consumer of the policy, not the policy itself.
+
+## Traceability
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+- [23e81ad8 [rule+skill] Rule-store sources for domain-store scaffolding instructions](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/23e81ad8-b67c-49af-97b5-f90f8bb0ae2c/ticket.toml)
+- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
+
+## Validation
+
+- Focused package tests for `spec-api`, `ticket-cli`, and `session-api` remain green.
+- Future scaffold generation work must add a template or fixture test that proves CRUD, schema validation, and nested workspace discovery without domain-specific patching.
*** End Patch

+# Summary
+
+Creating new domain stores repeatedly is still slow because the canonical bootstrap contract is scattered across existing domains. This spec now defines the minimal-store policy first, so later scaffolding or slash-skill automation can generate against a stable contract instead of guessing from prior implementations.
+
+## Behavior Story
+
+An engineer starting a new memory-backed domain store should be able to identify the smallest required handwritten surfaces, the shared `memory-api` capabilities they can reuse, the transport exposure pattern they need to follow, and the executable checks that prove the store works in a nested workspace with schema validation and cross-store references.
+
+## Provided Surface Contracts
+
+- The repository owns one canonical bootstrap policy for entity-store domains built on `memory_api::storage::EntityStore`.
+- The policy distinguishes entity-store domains from session-style artifact stores so scaffold work does not force both patterns into one template.
+- Every entity-store bootstrap must document: schema registration, store-open path, workspace-resolution path, transport exposure, schema-validation tests, and cross-store reference expectations.
+- Prompt-driven scaffolding is a downstream automation layer and must generate from this policy rather than inventing structure.
+
+## Bootstrap Comparison Matrix
+
+| Domain | Bootstrap primitive | Schema or config bootstrap | Transport or operator exposure | Validation anchor | Reuse signal |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| `ticket-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Built-in `TicketTypeSchema` values in `model/default_schema.rs`; workflow states and edge rules are domain-owned | CLI, MCP, and HTTP all expose the same store and schema surfaces; HTTP `schema.rs` is the clearest wire-format example | `tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/contracts_schema_validation.rs` plus transport parity work | Richest end-to-end precedent for schema, workflow, and transport parity |
+| `spec-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Single embedded `EntityTypeSchema` in `default_schema.rs` registered into a `SchemaRegistry` | CLI and MCP patterns stay close to the store with minimal extra workflow logic | `crates/spec-api/tests/schema_test.rs` | Closest example of the smallest entity-store bootstrap |
+| `rule-api` | Domain store over `memory_api` entity storage | Multiple embedded schemas registered in `rule_schema_registry()` | Operator-facing behavior stays store-centric and extends into generated-target workflows rather than a broad workflow engine | `crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs` and rule-store tests | Best example of a domain with more than one built-in entity schema |
+| `session-api` | Dedicated session artifact store and planning layer | `SessionStoreConfig` and `SessionStorePlan` derive paths and layout; no generic `SchemaRegistry` bootstrap | Hook binaries and MCP-side session workflows operate on session captures and artifacts instead of entity CRUD | `crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs` and capture-hook E2E tests | Distinct pattern; document it, but keep it out of the first entity-store template |
+
+## Template Boundary Decision
+
+### First template target
+
+The first canonical bootstrap template targets entity-store domains such as ticket, spec, rule, test, log, and similar stores that open through shared `memory-api` storage primitives.
+
+### Explicit non-goal for phase 1
+
+`session-api` remains a related but separate bootstrap pattern because it is config-driven artifact storage rather than a schema-registered entity store. The policy must reference it as a contrast case, not as a required output of the first template.
+
+### Handwritten vs generated boundary
+
+- Handwritten, domain-owned surfaces:
+  - entity schemas and semantic field rules
+  - domain store wrapper and query semantics
+  - cross-store reference vocabulary and evidence expectations
+  - transport-specific composition when a domain needs bespoke workflow or wire formats
+- Candidate generated surfaces:
+  - crate skeleton and manifest wiring
+  - default schema registration module
+  - store open/create shell using shared `memory-api` primitives
+  - CLI, MCP, and optional HTTP stub entry points
+  - baseline CRUD, schema-validation, and workspace-resolution tests
+
+## Required Validation
+
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p spec-api`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p ticket-cli --test contracts_schema_validation`
+- `cargo test --manifest-path memory-api/Cargo.toml -p session-api`
+- Focused workspace-resolution validation against the `memory-api` resolver path so new stores inherit nested-workspace behavior rather than re-implementing it.
+- At least one bootstrap fixture or template test must prove: create, get, update, delete, schema validation, and workspace-root discovery out of the box.
+
+## Related Implementation Tickets
+
+- [79dd2d35 [workflow-policy][memory-store] Research and define minimal domain-store bootstrap policy](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/79dd2d35-267b-4395-8316-0761df45f3c5/ticket.toml)
+- [39239e48 Transport-layer workspace-resolution parity (tracker)](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/39239e48-828a-41d8-a697-9cf02e980da9/ticket.toml)
+- [66fae806 [scaffold] Rule-generated store bootstrap instructions and slash command skill](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/66fae806-203d-4235-9151-4272eb0bb603/ticket.toml)
+
+## Background Knowledge References
+
+- `memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/model/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/default_schema.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`
+- `memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs`
+- `memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/schema.rs`
+
+## Legacy Content (Preserved)
+
+The original slash-skill and generated-guidance idea remains valid, but it is now downstream of the minimal-store policy. Generated prompt assets, drift checks, replay suites, and E2E scaffold testing should advance only after the repository agrees on the phase-1 entity-store template boundary defined above.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The repository has an explicit comparison matrix across `ticket-api`, `spec-api`, `rule-api`, and `session-api`.
+- The phase-1 template boundary clearly targets entity stores and explicitly excludes the session-artifact pattern.
+- Required bootstrap validation includes schema validation, CRUD, and nested-workspace resolution rather than compile-only checks.
+- Follow-up scaffold automation is described as a consumer of the policy, not the policy itself.
+
+## Traceability
+

+- [07d4b1b0 [skill] One-prompt domain-store scaffold slash command flow](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/.ticket/tickets/07d4b1b0-bc20-4ba7-98d4-ed09365f0437/ticket.toml)
+
+## Validation
+
+- Focused package tests for `spec-api`, `ticket-cli`, and `session-api` remain green.
+- Future scaffold generation work must add a template or fixture test that proves CRUD, schema validation, and nested workspace discovery without domain-specific patching.
